The Indus Valley civilization was centered on the Indus River basin, extending across what is today Pakistan and northwest India. Its major urban sites include Harappa, Mohenjo‑daro, Chanhu‑daro, Sutkagen‑dôr, Lothal, and others, spanning regions such as present‑day Punjab, Sindh, Rajasthan, Gujarat and Baluchistan.
